mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that simplifies the management of Kubernetes clusters through declarative APIs. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to provision, upgrade, and operate multiple clusters consistently across various infrastructures. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is necessary to access the Cluster API charts.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm in the 'capi-system' namespace.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --create-namespace --version v1.12.4

```

> Ensure the namespace is created for the components.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --create-namespace --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> You should see all pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this may lead to data loss of any clusters managed by CAPI.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace capi-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This removes the installed components.
> This removes the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster configurations. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -A -o yaml > cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> Backup your current cluster configurations.
> This upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Pod 'capi-controller-manager' is in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using: kubectl logs -n capi-system <pod-name> and fix any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to connect to the API server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the API server is down.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correct and the API server is running: kubectl cluster-info

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Machine" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Machine CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

versionNotes.changes: Fixed issues with Machine health checks and improved error messages.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see all Cluster API components running with 'kubectl get pods -n capi-system' showing all pods in the Running state.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --create-namespace --version v1.12.4

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and have kubectl and Helm installed.
